fr.name: Dialogue4LivingAreas
fr.description:
Les changements indispensables et les défis inévitables posés par l’atténuation du changement climatique et la nécessaire adaptation à ses effets, dans le contexte méditerranéen, nous obligent à modifier nos modèles anthropogéniques actuels, en décarbonisant les secteurs de notre économie de manière homogène, efficiente et équitable.

Le projet adoptera une approche holistique des Bassins de Vie Verts, notamment en promouvant l’efficacité énergétique, l’énergie renouvelable et les communautés citoyennes d’énergie, en soutenant les transports urbains durables, en améliorant la gestion de l’eau et des déchets, et en développant l’agriculture urbaine et péri-urbaine.

Tout en appuyant le projet de Communauté Thématique parallèle et la Euro-Med Academy, le projet propose de créer un mécanisme d’appui innovant pour les principales parties prenantes euro-méditerranéennes (autorités locales/nationales, institutions européennes, associations, réseaux, programmes de coopération, stratégies macro-régionales), visant à leur participation active dans une Communauté de Dialogue Institutionnel, pour renforcer la coopération méditerranéenne par l’amélioration et le transfert d’initiatives et de politiques publiques. 

Le projet D4LA s’articule donc autour de 3 objectifs:
-Mieux identifier les parties prenantes et les politiques publiques, et le potentiel de coopération,
-Encourager les échanges entre les différents niveaux institutionnels et soutenir le partage/transfert des expériences et pratiques,
-Améliorer la coordination stratégique aux niveaux national, européen et méditerranéen entre les principaux responsables des politiques publiques.

Les activités de ce projet de 7 ans formeront une arborescence, augmentant progressivement le nombre d’acteurs impliqués et le périmètre des actions, afin de mettre en place une Association Durable Euro-méditerranéenne pour le Dialogue Institutionnel sur les Bassins de Vie Verts, avec des résultats démontrés cohérents avec les objectifs 2030.
fr.expected_achievements:
The specific objective is the identification, strengthening, and commitment of 120 key public stakeholders that will, through the capacity building support mechanism, directly contribute to the sharing and transferring of thematic projects outputs.
The transfer of practices and results to other stakeholders and territories and their integration into multi-level policies and strategies will be achieved based on the multidisciplinary expertise of the partnership and through the EUCLL activities.
The Working Community/Association will establish long-term cooperation between 170 key public stakeholders for a sustainable increased coordination and amplification of public initiatives, policies and strategies at Euro-Mediterranean level.
fr.actual_achievements:
On the administrative side:

LP coordinated the project review process, preparing a proposal with the partnership and presenting it to the JS during the review meeting in January 2025. This included a shift of focus of the project activities, to concentrate mainly on the Institutional Policy Dialogues as main output of the project. It also included a partner modification: NALAS replaces AEE as IPA country partner, due to the lack of investment of AEE in the project activities. This process lasted for the duration of the semester, due to the negotiations with the partners and the JS regarding project activities and budget, and the administrative constraints of the partnership modification.

In addition, LP coordinated the submission of the fourth project report, with the payment claim submitted in March 2025.

On the technical side:

A partnerhsip meeting was held in Nice on 22-23 January 2025, with the main focus of validating the project amendment proposal to be made to the JS in the frame of the project review. Discussions focused on the shift of activities granting the Institutional Policy Dialogue a central role in the project, the consequent budget shifts, and the partnership modification. The new partner NALAS was proposed by REGEA, who initiated contacts and negotiations.  The meeting also focused on the roadmap for the years 2025 and 2026, and the project timeline and main deadlines and events of the year 2025 (and tentatively 2026) were set. 

The first event organised by the partnership was the joint session of the Thematic Wokring Groups, held online on 27 February 2025, bringing together the Thematic Projects to present the new roadmap of the Institutional Policy Dialogue process, and notably the EUCLID Policy Hub being set up by the project. 

In addition, a First Core Group meeting, was held online on 4 April 2025, bringing together external key Euro-Mediterranean stakeholders, to be involved in the EUCLID Policy Hub and to advise the Thematic Projects on bringing their results to policy level.

A second Core Group meeting was held on 18 June 2025, online, to prepare the second Institutional Policy Dialogue, which will be held on 15-16 October 2025 in Brussels, in the frame of the EU week of Regions and Cities.

From March onwards, the partners focused on preparing the yearly event in Brussels, working on preparing the agenda, logistics, as well as the content (speakers, role of TPs, role of Core Group members, etc.). A save the date and then invitations were sent to the TPs and Core Group members in June 2025. 

A call was published to the attention of the Thematic Projects, for them to propose results they have developped which could be presented as policy champions during the second Institutional Policy Dialogue. Following this process, three projects were selected to be policy champions. Discussions with the projects on the expectations were held during the following months leading to the second Institutional Policy Dialogue and are still ongoing. 

In addition, the partners participated in the Thematic Community Project's annual event, held in Barcelona on 28-29 May 2025. In this instance, the project was responsible for organising and delivering a training focused on policy. This training was delivered by a staff member of POLIS. 

The LP and WP leaders also organised and led monthly coordination meetings with the work package leaders of the Thematic Community Project, to keep each project update of the other one's activities, and notably to prepare the training session for the Barcelona event. In parallel, monthly communication meetings were held between D4LA and C4LA communication teams, to smoothly implement a cohesive and comprehensive communication strategy.

en.name: Dialogue4LivingAreas
en.description:
The necessary changes and inevitable challenges of the mitigation of climate change and the adaptation to its effects, especially in the Mediterranean context, require us to modify the prevailing anthropogenic model, challenging us to decarbonize the economy in all its sectors in a homogenous, efficient and fair manner.

From a thematic point of view, the project will focus on a holistic approach to “green living areas”, notably: promotion of energy efficiency, renewable and citizen energy communities; support of sustainable urban transports; improvement of waste and water management; development of urban and peri-urban agriculture.

While supporting the parallel Thematic Community Project and the Euro-Med Academy, for a maximum efficiency of the overall process from local to transnational level, the proposal aims to implement an innovative support mechanism for key Euro-Mediterranean stakeholders (local /national authorities, European institutions, associations, networks, cooperation programmes, macroregional strategies), to actively participate in a Euro-med working Community for green Living areas’ Institutional Dialogue, to reinforce cooperation in the Mediterranean area by improving and transferring public initiatives and policies.

D4LA thus proposes 3 main axes:
-To better identify public stakeholders, public policies and the potential for coordination (WP1 Reuse),
-To foster exchanges between these different institutional layers and support sharing/transfer of experiences and practices (WP2 Transfer),
-To improve strategic coordination at national, European and Mediterranean levels with key policy players (WP3 Coordination).

The activities of this 7-year project will form an arborescence, gradually increasing the number of actors involved and broadening the scope of the actions, by implementing a sustainable Euro-Mediterranean Green Living Areas’ Institutional Dialogue Association with mature results in line with the 2030 targets.
en.expected_achievements:
The specific objective is the identification, strengthening, and commitment of 120 key public stakeholders that will, through the capacity building support mechanism, directly contribute to the sharing and transferring of thematic projects outputs.
The transfer of practices and results to other stakeholders and territories and their integration into multi-level policies and strategies will be achieved based on the multidisciplinary expertise of the partnership and through the EUCLL activities.
The Working Community/Association will establish long-term cooperation between 170 key public stakeholders for a sustainable increased coordination and amplification of public initiatives, policies and strategies at Euro-Mediterranean level.
en.actual_achievements:
The main objective of the first semester was to get the project going and the partnership involved. In order to ensure cohesive partner collaboration, monthly conference calls were set up, for the partners to get to know each other and to clarify the project structure and the expected achievmeents. A steering committee (SC) was set up and a first SC meeting held adopting the project rules. 

A first project meeting was also held in person in Nice (France) on 3-4 April 2023. This allowed the partners to meet in person, and to clarify various issues (definition of Green Living Areas, objectives and characteristics of the Working Community/Association, communication issues, etc.).

The first period focused on the finalisation of the administrative issues: signature of Partnership Agreement, of Subsidy Contract, setting up of JEMS accesses and clarification of the programme manual rules (Basecamp, reporting, expenses certification, etc). 

The communication activities started with the preparation and submission of the Mission's Communication Strategy (currently being reviewed following comments from the JTS).

In terms of technical activities, work started on WP1 with the mapping of stakeholders, associations and events, with all partners providing previous input for the activity piloted by PP2 IREC. This work in still ongoing in semester 2. 

Finally, the project contributed to the preparation of the Programme event to be held in Ljubljana (Slovenia) on 21-22 November 2023. It also organised, in parallel, an Institutional Dialogue Projects’ Session, held on 21 November 2023 in the morning, bringing together the four institutional dialogue projects to discuss common issues.

LP1 ensured a smooth start and endeavoured to create a cohesive partnership collaboration, by organising and leading monthly partnership conference calls, to ensure a common understanding of the project activities and programme expectations. All partners participated actively in all Project Meetings and JS coordinated events.

The administrative management of the project was ensured by LP1, who organised a first in person meeting held in Nice on 3-4 March 2023, and a second in person meeting was held in the frame of the Programme event "Connecting the Dots", organised in Ljubljana on 21-22 November 2023. These permitted more in-depth discussions on the implementation of project activities, resulting in clear roadmaps for the next steps .

LP1 also oversaw the drafting of the communication strategy by the communication partners (PP4 POLIS and PP7 ANETEL), again in collaboration with their counterparts from C4LA. 

LP1 led the collaboration efforts with the TCP, the newly selected TPs (from November 2023 onwards), and the other IDPs, in order to ensure a common understanding of the project activities and programme expectations.  

The initial conceptualisation of the EUCLID Working Community/Association started, notably with the mapping of stakeholders for the core group, and the creation of the storytelling by the communication partners. This initial work has enabled D4LA to prepare the grounds for the First Institutional Dialogue, to be held in Brussels in October 2024, to coincide with the European Week of Regions and Cities.

Focus was directed towards deploying key tasks of WP1: defining and collecting initial data on outcomes from prior projects and programs, identifying crucial stakeholders associated with Green Living Areas (GLA) and governance in the Med, and delineating essential policy instruments for promoting GLA throughout the Med region. A collaborative approach was adopted, enabling the sharing and validation of insights and proposals with project partners and the TCP of the Green Living Areas mission. The systematic development of activities 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3, relied on existing knowledge and previous experiences, with a special emphasis on the local scale and aiming to assist the next future new Thematic Projects in translating their outcomes into policies. Once the first set of Thematic Projects was selected in November 2023, IREC coordinated the mapping work to include stakeholders from the TP project partners and associated partners,to involve them in the governance process. 

Despite the focus on TPs, it's important to highlight that external stakeholders relevant to the GLA in the Med area have been included in the mapping activities, with a view of implicating them in the Institutional Dialogue process.

Implementation of WP2 started towards the end of the current reporting period (period 2).
UPatras coordinated and launched the survey of this activity for the Public transversal Euro-Mediterranean Living Lab the main aim of which is to record the multilevel experiences via a questionnaire. The survey was discussed and validated by the partnership, then released in the public during the “Connecting the Dots” event which was organized by JS in Slovenia in November 2023. After that, the questionnaire was shared to all governance and thematic projects and it was published online in the Mission website and on Basecamp. 

The report which records the survey results will be finalized in the next semester after the engagement of the public partners of the selected thematic projects. The report will aim to provide recommendations on how to best address the needs of institutions, reflecting the wide range of level of experience across institutions, covering the entire Euro-MED area and highlighting specificities and classifying recommendations accordingly. 

Additionally, UPatras started drafting the EUCLL methodology based on its previous experiences in Interreg Med and ENI CBC MED initiatives. Thus, a customized integrated LL methodology is being developed to encourage the transfer of practices and results to other stakeholders and territories, and their integration into local, regional, national and European policies and strategies. As agreed in the partners meeting in Slovenia in November 2023, UPatras made efforts to draft the methodology by the end of January 2024, when it will be presented to the partners for comments/suggestions and final approval. This methodology will be the tool for the implementation of the policy dialogues among the identified stakeholders and the thematic projects representatives.

Following the attempt to integrate a partner’s certificate that was long to issue, combined with a technical bug in Jems that will be only solve in a later version, we have to submit 2 separated project report : one for all partners except PP8 LEN, one for PP8 LEN only. Yet the workplan progress related content is included in this very report for all partners, including PP8 LEN.

Yet the workplan progress related content is included for all partners, including PP8 LEN, in the first report (PR.2).
WP1
Under the coordination of IREC (PP2), and with participation and contributions from all the partners, significant efforts were dedicated on the mapping activities, concentrating on the identification and collection of data related to outcomes from both past and newly selected thematic projects. In parallel, the partnership continued working on the mapping of stakeholders involved in Green Living Areas (GLA) and governance in the Mediterranean, and of policy instruments promoting GLA across the Mediterranean. These efforts contribute directly to the development and implementation of Institutional Dialogues (WP3) using the Living Labs methodology (WP2) and support the establishment of a future GLA entity, facilitating the translation of outcomes into policies for the ongoing and upcoming Thematic Projects.
These mapping activities were done in coordination with C4LA to avoid duplicate work and to ensure a coordination amongst the mission. 
Following this collaborative work, IREC was able to complete and submit for validation the deliverables 1.1 (toolkit of TP outcomes for public initiatives and policies – factsheets of selected outcomes), 1.2 (public stakeholder clusterisation) and 1.3 (transversal mapping at Euro-Mediterranean level).

WP2
During the reference period, as a first step in the implementation of the Living Lab methodology, UPatras reported the recommendations from the multi-layer survey which included the analysis of survey data providing detailed insights into respondents' views on GLA initiatives and their involvement in policy-making processes. Based on that, UPatras updated the Public transversal Euro-Mediterranean Living Lab Methodology and reported it. The guidelines adapt past methodologies to the needs of Green Living Areas, ensuring effective policy dialogues. Together, these deliverables lay the groundwork for enhancing public involvement in environmental policy-making and developing a comprehensive framework for Living Labs in the Euro-Mediterranean region.
This methodology was then applied to the organisation of five Online Thematic Working Groups (TWG), focusing on policy, held on 11-13 June 2024, focusing respectively on green mobility (TWG1), green energy systems (TWG2), green & climate change resilient spatial planning (TWG3), financing the green transition (TWG4) and green community engagement (TWG5). The preparation and management of the 5 TWGs was a collaborative effort of the partnership, with each of the specific sessions lead by different partners (TWG1 led by POLIS and UPatras, TWG2 led by IREC and LEN, TWG3 led by REGEA and UPatras, TWG4 led by IRE, IREC and REGEA, and TWG5 led by ANETEL and LEN.
These TWG were defined in coordination with C4LA in the context of the collaboration on synergies. Common working group titles were defined, and it was agreed during the Turin Thematic Community kick-off meeting that specific policy-oriented TWGs would be organised by D4LA in preparation for the first Institutional Dialogue to be held in Brussels on 10 October 2024. 

WP3
LP1 ensured the management and the overall coordination of project activities, by organising monthly conference calls with the partners.
A 3rd in person partnership meeting and Steering Committee was held on 12-13 February 2024 in Nice. This was the occasion to discuss the mapping activities, as well as validate the Living Lab methodology proposed by UPatras and define the next steps. It was also the occasion to prepare D4LA’s participation in the Thematic Community’s kick-off meeting.
The partnership also participated in the Thematic Community’s KOM held in Turin on 10-11 April 2024. This was the occasion for D4LA to have an in-person coordination meeting with C4LA, but also to co-organise the first thematic working groups, and to present its policy-focused work and the first Institutional Dialogue during a dedicated session. A second in person meeting of the partnership was also held in this occasion.
In addition, the partners, under the leadership of LP1, started the process of preparing the first Institutional Dialogue, and set a reverse schedule leading up to this event to be held on 10 October 2024. 
In parallel, LP1 and the WP leaders coordinated with their C4LA counterparts to propose to the JS a consolidated table of synergies between their respective activities. Many meetings were held on this topic, and the final agreed “synergies table” was agreed upon by both governance projects during an in person meeting in Marseille in early July 2024. 
During this period, the partners shared the task of attending the KOMs of the newly selected TPs, and taking this occasion to present the work of the Institutional Dialogue Project and highlight how it can be of assistance to the TPs in their achieving their policy-oriented objectives. This workload was shared amongst the partners based on their geographic location, their contacts with various projects/LPs, and their interests in specific technical topics. 
Year 2 of the project was the start of the concrete project activities.

Indeed, after preparing the groups in year 1 with the mapping activities, and preparation of the necessary methodologies and frameworks, year 2 enabled the project to truly implement the activities.

As such, the Thematic Workign Groups (TWGs) were organised in collaboration with C4LA for a smooth and clear communication with the Thematic Projects. The first set of TWGs were organised and led jointly by C4LA and D4LA, in the frame of the Community Kick off meeting held in Turin in March 2024. The 4 TWGs were co-led by a partner from each project, based on their specific expertise and preference. The second set of TWGs, this time focusing on policy, were held online in June 2024, with the main objective to prepare the first Institutional Policy Dialogue.

The main event of D4LA for the year 2024 was the organisation of the first Institutional Policy Dialogue in Brussels in October 2024. This event brought together over 60 stakeholders, including partners and associated partners of the Thematic Projects, as well as some selected key Euro-Mediterranean stakeholders. During this event 4 policy champions were presented and discussed, for potential replication, adaptation and transfer.  On the basis of these discussions, policy briefs were prepared and finalised in early 2025.

fr.name: Communauté Thématique Bassins de Vie Verts
fr.description:
Le changement climatique (CC) modifie l’habitat et le cycle de vie des organismes. La vie dans la biosphère est aujourd'hui plus vulnérable que jamais, et les territoires méditerranéens en seront sévèrement affectés sur le plan environnemental, social et économique. Le CC nécessite une vision systémique et holistique, définissant des solutions d'atténuation et d'adaptation intégrées. Une gouvernance stratégique basée sur la coopération et la coordination est nécessaire.
Le défi de la Communauté Thématique Bassins de Vie Verts (CT BVV) est d'encourager ces mécanismes de gouvernance, par le renforcement des capacités et le transfert de connaissances. Notre proposition consiste en un système de gouvernance permettant la réplication des résultats de manière efficace dans les territoires MED et suivant une approche transnationale.
Le mécanisme de gouvernance comprend une coordination à 3 niveaux: 1) au sein de la CT BVV; 2) au sein du programme Interreg Euro-Med et des projets de gouvernance; 3) avec des réseaux, des organisations stratégiques et des décideurs clés pour assurer l'impact d'amplification dans toute la région.
Les principaux résultats sont: 1) des outils et méthodologies prêts à l’emploi pour les autorités locales et régionales; 2) un processus de co-création de laboratoires vivants assurant la liaison entre la CT BVV et la société civile, les programmes et initiatives de l'UE, des pays et des régions.
À cette fin, la proposition adapte et intègre les meilleures pratiques de 2 projets Interreg MED: Energy Renouvelables et Transports Urbains, en les adaptant à la CT BVV. L'innovation du projet réside dans la mise en œuvre de 2 processus de mainstreaming: 1) axé sur le transfert des résultats aux autorités publiques; 2) axé sur les actions encouragées par la société civile et les partenariats public-privé. Le projet vise à intégrer une approche transdisciplinaire du bien-être des personnes et des opportunités socio-économiques et financières.

en.name: Green Living Areas Thematic Community
en.description:
Climate Change (CC) is altering the habitats and life cycles of organisms. Life in the biosphere is today more vulnerable than ever, and Mediterranean territories will be affected severely by this environmentally, socially and economically. CC requires a systemic and holistic vision (IPCC March 2022), which allows defining solutions for mitigation and adaptation in an integrated manner. It is necessary strategic governance based on cooperation and coordination, taking firm steps forward in the right direction. 

The GLA thematic community's challenge is to encourage governance mechanisms that speed up the implementation of CC mitigation and adaptation solutions, through capacity building and knowledge transfer. Our proposal consists of a governance system enabling the replicability of the results in an effective way in Med cities and towns and following a transnational approach.

The governance mechanism includes coordination at 3 levels: 1) within GLA thematic community; 2) within the Interreg Euro-Med Programme and Governance Projects; 3) with networks, strategic organisations, and key decision-makers to ensure the amplification impact in the whole region.

The main outputs are: 1) tools and methodologies ready to be implemented by local and regional authorities; 2) living labs co-creation process liaising the GLA community with civil society, EU, Countries and Region programmes and initiatives.
To this end, the proposal adapts and integrates the best practices of 2 Interreg MED projects: Renewable Energy and Urban Transports, adapting them to the new challenge of promoting GLA. 

The innovation of our project lies in the fact that we will implement 2 mainstreaming processes: 1) focused on the transfer of results to public authorities; 2) focused on actions encouraged by civil society and public-private partnerships. In this way, our proposal seeks to incorporate a transdisciplinary approach to people's well-being, socio-economic and financial opportunities.
en.expected_achievements:
Obtain a set of transferable and replicable tools and outcomes from the thematic projects, to be used in the further phases, through the creation of a strong community with synergies and cooperation among the projects, and a clear common direction.

Contribute to better governance in the Med area through the transferring of the community results and their integration into policies. 
Achieve jointly developed solutions with multi-level actors, ensuring society involvement through systemic thinking/co-creation approach to tackle governance challenges regarding climate change adaptation, citizens' well-being and green transition.

fr.name: Dialogue4Innovation
fr.description:
Le projet Dialogue4Innovation contribue au développement d’une meilleure gouvernance et d’une économie plus innovante et durable en Méditerranée, en y associant un large réseau d'organisations issues du secteur public, des entreprises, de la recherche et de la société civile. Le renforcement des capacités est l'un des piliers du projet, car il est essentiel d'accroître la capacité des décideurs politiques et des parties prenantes à répondre plus efficacement aux objectifs de développement durable. En travaillant avec les acteurs territoriaux par le biais de laboratoires de politiques d’innovation transformative (TIPL), le projet favorisera la diffusion d'approches plus systémiques et intégrées, nécessaires pour relever les défis urgents de la transition écologique. Le projet contribuera à améliorer les conditions durables d'un dialogue institutionnel et social permanent en Méditerranée, tout en mêlant dimension transnationale et solutions locales, et en augmentant la coordination, la cohérence des politiques et l'alignement des actions pour une économie durable innovante. 
En collaboration avec la communauté thématique de la Mission, Dialogue4Innovation proposera une plateforme pour mobiliser les parties prenantes méditerranéennes afin d'encourager une gouvernance plus ouverte, inclusive et efficace, en augmentant la visibilité et la pertinence des instances méditerranéennes aux niveaux européen et international. Afin d’amplifier son impact, le projet soutiendra les alliances multi-acteurs axées sur les défis MED.
 
Pour atteindre ses objectifs, le projet Dialogue4Innovation s'appuiera sur les résultats des projets thématiques Interreg Euro-MED, en réduisant l'écart avec les priorités politiques - au niveau local, régional, national, méditerranéen et européen - et en favorisant leur intégration dans les politiques et pratiques structurelles.

en.name: Dialogue4Innovation
en.description:
The Dialogue4Innovation Project is contributing to a better governance and to a more innovative and sustainable economy in the Mediterranean, engaging with a large network of organisations from public sector, business, research and civil society. Capacity building is one of the pillars of the Project, since it is key to increase the capacity of policy makers and stakeholders to address the Sustainable Development Goals more effectively. By working with territorial actors through Transformative innovation policy labs (TIPL), the project will promote the diffusion of more systemic and integrated approaches, which are necessary to tackle the urgent challenges of the ecological transition. The Project will contribute to improve the long-lasting conditions for a permanent institutional and social dialogue in the Mediterranean, bridging the transnational dimension with local solutions and increasing coordination, policy coherence and alignment of actions for an innovative sustainable economy. 
Jointly with the Mission thematic community, Dialogue4Innovation will offer a platform to mobilise Mediterranean stakeholders to foster more open, inclusive and effective governance, increasing the visibility and relevance of the Mediterranean instances at the European and international levels. To amplify its impact, the Project will support MED challenge-driven multi-actor alliances.
 
To meet its objectives, the Dialogue4Innovation Project will build on the outcomes of the Interreg Euro-Med thematic projects, narrowing the gap with the policy priorities – at local, regional, national, Mediterranean and European level – and promoting their integration in structural policies and practices.
en.expected_achievements:
To narrow the gap between thematic projects outcomes and policy priorities, through a more systemic and integrated mapping and visualisation of innovation projects’ outcomes, collaboration networks and policy priorities in the Mission area
To create more favourable conditions for the absorption of project results into policies and practices in Med region, at different territorial levels, contributing to more integrated policy making and to the acceleration of sustainability transition.
Improving long-lasting conditions of a permanent institutional and social dialogue to bridge the transnational dimension with local solutions, by increasing coordination, policy coherence and alignment of actions for an innovative sustainable economy
en.actual_achievements:
The Dialogue4Innovation project, officially started on 1° January 2023, during the first period of activities has been successfully started-up advancing along 3 main lines of work:
1.	Set-up of the project governance and management structure: Project partners met for the first time already on 17 January 2023 in Marseille in person/online to discuss the project rational and workplan in the frame of the Programme architecture to create a general alignment in the consortium. The Steering Committee was formally set-up during the kick-off meeting on 14 February 2023 (online). The partnership agreement was finally signed on 3rd April 2023 and the subsidy contract between the Programme MA and the LP (on behalf of the partnership) was signed on 28 April 2023. The Steering Committee met again on 20 June 2023 to discuss project advancements, plan activities and prepare for technical and administrative first reporting. Day to day management and communication is kept through a dedicate workspace in Microsoft Teams, while communication with the Euro4governance group and the JS are kept through Basecamp.
2.	Coordination and alignment with Community4Innovation: the alignment with the TCP, under the guidance of the JS, required substantial effort despite previous contacts anticipated after projects approval. A 2-years joint/aligned workplan has been defined and sent to the JS at the begin of June, highlighting activities in common between the two Projects. A joint communication strategy has been drafted and the joint initial communication channels have been created (website and social media accounts, templates). The Mission official launch event was organised and scheduled at the begin of July;
3.	Creation and broadening of the stakeholder network: in parallel with the fine tuning of the internal organisation Project partnership has already started liaising with other relevant strategies and initiatives and building the stakeholders network. The organisation of the first innovation camp together with EUSAIR Presidency and IPA ADRION Programme, within the 8th EUSAIR Forum on 23-24 May created a big momentum for the new project. Representatives of three different EU Commission DGs (Regio, Mare and JRC) participated as speakers at the event, together with representatives of Programmes, national and regional administrations, universities and research organisations. One of the follow-ups is the close dialogue with the Regional Cooperation Council, together with the JS. The Project/Mission was presented in several public events by Partners, such as the SeaRICA workshop on blue economy (25/01), CPMR IMC Working Group and General Assembly (22/02, 29-30/06), Dialogue4Tourism (02/03)Western Balkans Innovation & Research Platform (08/06), MedCat Days (13/06), WestMed stakeholder conference (22/06).  Special attention has been given to youth involvement, inviting MYC representative in the innovation camp to start a possible collaboration;
Besides Project level activities, Project Partners, mainly through the LP in this initial phase, actively contributed to all Programme common activities, coordinated by the JS, and to the start-up of the overall coordination mechanism and respective activities. On the other hand, each Partner organised internally with the definition of the working team. Each Partner appointed a contact person who is active in the project, while the teams will evolve according to the workplan schedule considering that most of « technical » activities are supposed to start in 2024 and 2025.


During the reporting period the Dialogue4Innovation partnership worked to consolidate the management structure established in the first half of the year, to continue the alignement with C4I and to strenghten the coordination within the Euro-MED4Governance architecture.
Concerning the first strand, after the approval of the 2-year joint workplan WP and Activity Leaders worked to plan their respective duties in more detail. Priority was given to activities ready to start and less dependent on information concerning the thematic projects (approved only at the end of November 2023).  The partners met bilaterally online and in presence and and a plenary technical meeting was held on 15 December 2023. The progress of Activities is summarised in the next section.
A minor adaptation of the Application Form (now in version 4.0) was approved by written procedure by the Project Steering Committee.

Concenrning the alignment with C4I, it focused on two activities which are jointly implemented by the two Projects: the implementation of the joint Communication Strategy (A 1.5) and the set-up of the ISCE Hub (A 3.1) as it is illusrated in more details in the workplan progress section. The two projects jointly contributed to the organisation of the following events:
- The launch event of the Innovative Sustainable Economy Mission, which took place on the 6th of July 2023 (online), gathering representatives of the partner organizations, the Interreg Euro-MED Programme, the Union for the Mediterranean (UfM) and the European Commission
-  the workshop "Starting the journey together: Co-designing the mission scope and identifying priority challenges on Innovative Sustainable Economy" during the "Connecting the Dots" Programme event (21-22 November 2023, Ljubljana, Slovenia) to consolidate Mission shared understanding and common narrative among the partners and closer stakeholders;
- "MED Talks" (5 December 2023, online) organised with UfM and the other 3 Mission governance projects, specifically targeting stakeholders from the southern Mediterranean shore.


Concerning the coordination within the Euro-MED4Governance, the partnership actively contributed to the activities of the working group supporting the organisation of the Programme event "Connecting the Dots" for the design of different sessions. In parallel, D4I with D4GLA promoted the coordination among the 4 IDPs for the organisation of a common side event to the Programme event titled “Interreg Euro-MED Missions for a Mediterranean Green Deal.  Effective synergies: starting with the right foot” (21 November 2023). The session was participated by several Mediterranean governance organisations and EU Institutions (either in presence or online) and helped consolidating the reciprocal relationships. 
They have been important steps to test the coordination among the different Missions' governance projects, that needs to be consolidated in the coming months.


During the period, Projects partners contributed to dissemination (re)presentating the project in several external events, such as: the EUSAIR event "Towards the future of blue growth in the Adriatic and Ionian Basin" (7 July 2023, Rimini, Italy), the final event of the Interreg Adrion 2014-2020 BLUEAIR project (11 July 2023, Trieste, Italy), the 14th UfM Working Group on Blue Economy (11 October 2023, Barcelona, Spain).
During the reporting period, the project moved out of its start-up phase and some activities began to be defined more concretely, in accordance with the workplan:

-Concerning WP1 a first internal draft of the mapping of policy priorities in the Mediterranean (A1.1) has been developed having in mind coordination with the clustering of projects in the thematic community and articulation of the ISEC Hub. Given the great diversity among existing strategies and agendas, the mapping exercise is exploring different possibilities to provide effective and usable information.
Concerning the development of the monitoring system for MED collaboration networks (A1.2) an application for a targeted analysis has been submitted to the ESPON Programme at the end of March 2024, which has been approved for funding. The targeted analysis, which reflects very closely the original proposal in the D4I AF, will allow to upscale and broaden the analysis going beyond the scope of the Mission ISE and the Euro-MED programme area. Moreover, the proposal has been submitted involving a number of Mediterranean stakeholder and other Interreg Programme, going in a direction of a truly joint developed solution, going beyond cooperation with the TCP. The initiative has been also presented at the MedLab Annual meeting (26 June 2024, online) upon invitation from Interact.

- Regarding WP2, although most of the activities will be implemented in the coming semesters, a further step towards sharing the approach to transformative innovation, the cornerstone of the project, was taken through the second Innovation Camp (28-29 May, Barcelona, A.3.3). A joint contribution to the Euro-MED Academy consultation was developed in collaboration with C4I.

- Finally, regarding WP3, the ISEC Hub structuring process continued. A public consultation workshop was organised (26 March 2024, online) to gather feedback from the Hub Ambassadors and further develop the envisaged tools. Important steps were also taken to consolidate the Mission Coordination Bodies (A3.2) with the organisation of the first Mission Consultative Body (MCB) meeting during the MedCat Days (25 June 2024, Barcelona and online). As anticipated the second edition of the Innovation camp has been organised successful with good level of participation and satisfaction. 
Moreover, having cleared picture of the structuring of the Mission protagonists (TPs) and relationships, the table of contents liasing for advocacy strategy has been drafted in parallel with the start-up of the activity to explore and map the existence of multi stakeholder alliances to jointly challenge sustainable development challenges at Med level.

Coordination with C4I was effective. The two projects exchanged regularly on joint activities and also on the topic of TP clustering. In addition, D4I participated in some of the KoMs organised by the projects, in the LP seminar (14-15 February 2024, online) as well as in the official launch of the ISE Community of Practice    (CoP) and the MED Innovation Day (18-19 June 2024, Marseille).

During the reporting period, the project entered a new phase and the activities launched according to the work plan took qualitative steps forward. In addition, the governance structure established in the first two years and the coordination with C4I, the Programme and the Euro-MED4 Governance Group were strengthened. 

- Concerning WP1, during the reporting period the interim version of the mapping of policy priorities in the Mediterranean (A.1.1, D.1.1.1) was completed. The document contains a first review of the main strategies and framework and the regional level and a categorization of the based on the Mission sectoral priorities. Significant developments also affected monitoring system (A.1.2). Following the approval of the TA MedCoopNet by the ESPON Programme, D4I contributed to the drafting of the ToR of the study  and to the assessment of the bids that led to the awarding before the end of 2024.  

- During the reporting period, some of the activities of WP2 have started to become more concrete as the time for implementation of the TIPLs (A.2.2) approaches. A working session (3 December 2024, online) with partners to identify challenges and relevant stakeholders to be selected was carried out, and a set of tools to facilitate the implementation of the labss is under development. Moreover, partners in charge of A.2.1 advanced in the development of the conceptual framework, which will set the ground for the TIPLs. The project, through the selected representatives, continued to follow the ongoing work related to the Euro-MED Academy.  

- Finally, with regard to WP3, the launch of the ISEC Hub (2 October 2024, online) marked a new milestone for the ISE Mission. The event gathered around 90 participants and provided an opportunity to exchange detailed information on the activities foreseen by the four pillars of the Hub, which currently counts 234 members from all over the Mediterranean region. In addition, on the external relations side of the project, the Liasing for Advocacy strategy was finalized (A.3.5), identifying a number of stakeholders with broad decision-making capacity in the field of ISE and in relation to the MED area. Lastly, preliminary reflexions on A.3.6 began through alignment of work in the L4A strategy with that of the policy messages and their future development and dissemination. A working group was set up for this purpose.

Coordination with C4I remained effective. The two projects had regular exchanges on joint activities and worked together to strengthen the network of the Mission within the ISE CoP. In this context, D4I actively contributed through the LP to the organisation and facilitation of the 2nd ISE CoP meeting (14 November 2024 online), and also coordinated a masterclass on the processes of amplification of project results. Another important opportunity to present the Mission's work was the Infoday on the 5th Call of Euro-MED 2021-2027, organised by the Italian NCP, where the LP emphasised the complementarity of actions between IDPs and TCPs (18 July 2024, online). 

During the reporting period, D4I actively contributed to the activities of the Euro-MED4Governance group. It participated in the two Euro4Governance meetings organised by the JS (3-4 July 2024, Marseille - 13 November 2024, online). It also joined the Library and the Events WGs. In addition, the LP took the opportunity to participate in some meetings organised by the other Missions, such as: (i) the first Institutional Dialogue of the EUCLID Working Community organised by the GLA Mission (10 October 2024, Bruxelles); (ii) the meeting of the MED Cluster Innovation and Technology Capacities organised by C4T project (15 November 2024, online). Finally, ERR actively contributed to the organisation and facilitation of the first Italian Euro-MED Partners Forum (21 November 2024, Florence, Italy). 
During the reporting period, Dialogue4Innovation continued its implementation, following the path set out in the previous semester and made significant progress for the launch of its core activities according to the newly approved Application form and two-year workplan.

- Concerning WP1, the implementation of the TA MedCoopNet, funded by the ESPON Programme, continued as planned, with some D4I partners involved in the SC alongside the project's LP. The selected provider started the analysis developing a taxonomy for mapping collaborative projects against various societal challenges and thematic classifications and setting-up the database of projects. The activity is strongly linked to D4I since the system under development will be updated by the project.

- Activities in WP2 also made significant progress during the semester. The methodology for implementing the Transformative Innovation Policy Labs - TIP Labs - (A.2.1) was finalised by GENCAT, with the support of TGPT, and presented to the partnership. Each partner began identifying the place-based challenges at the heart of their TIP Lab and seeking out experts to contribute to their implementation.

- Activities under WP3 regularly progressed with great results. Following the establishment and launch of the ISEC Hub (A.3.1) in the previous semester, the ISE partners involved (AIE, MedWaves and MIO-ECSDE) continued to develop the tools offered (KUMU Directory, LinkedIn Group, etc.) under the guidance of CPMR and BETA TC, co-chairing the activity. Furthermore, a rebranding plan and a new engagement plan for the Hub members were developed. 
GENCAT and TGPT also carried out significant work within the WP to organise the upcoming Innovation Camp (A.3.3), which is being co-organised with the European Commission's Joint Research Centre (JRC). The Camp will provide an opportunity to enhance understanding and application of the transformative innovation approach to addressing complex challenges, which is a cornerstone of D4I. 
Considerable progress has also been made in terms of liasing for advocacy (A.3.5) under the direction of CPRM thanks to the planning of a strategic initiative of cross-fertilization synergizing the ISE Mission with the Ocean & Waters Mission of Horizon Europe (BlueMissionMed project). Furthermore, the joint Working Group on liasing and advocacy (ERR, CPMR, MRDEUF, MEDWAVES, AIE, DEI and C4I-BETA TC) started also monitoring some Euro-Mediterranean institutional actors and initiatives to identify also other potential liaising opportunities. 
Finally, with regard to A.3.6, the partners collaborated to determine the strategic direction of the D4I policy messages and select topics for the initial policy paper concerning the future of the Mediterranean region post-2027.

Coordination with Community4Innovation remained effective, reinforced by regular communication and ongoing efforts to stabilise the structure developed over the past years. The two projects regularly exchanged information on joint activities and collaborated to strengthen the Mission network within the ISE CoP. D4I actively contributed to organising and facilitating the third ISE CoP meeting (2-4 April 2025, Sarajevo). Within this framework, the LP contributed to the following: (i) developing and moderating a session on opportunities for cooperation in developing policy-oriented knowledge; and (ii) developing and moderating an interactive session to identify opportunities for cooperation with the TPs in terms of communication.

Communication activities were regularly carried out in cooperation with C4I. During the reporting period ERR contributed to: (i) creating and launching the Innovative Sustainable Economy (ISE) Communication Network, gathering together both the Governance and the Thematic projects of the Mission (29 May 2025, online); (ii) coordinating and publishing the fourth edition of the ISE Mission's newsletter; (iii) updating the ISE Mission’s website; iv) organising meetings for the management of the activity; (v) introducing the work of the Mission and the ISE Communication Network at the Welcome Webinar organised by the Mission for projects selected through Call-04 and at the KoMs of CirBioWaste (13 June 2025, online), Sole MED (18–19 June 2025, Ferrara) and RECONNECT (23 June 2025, online).

In addition to what has been reported above, D4I continued to coordinate with the Euro-MED4Governance Group throughout the semester. It actively contributed to the group's activities by participating in the regular meeting organised by the JS (24 April 2025, online), and by guiding the process for developing an IDPs session to be hosted during the Programme's event in Varna, Bulgaria, in November 2025. Finally, the project also explored synergies with other Interreg programmes, particularly Interreg Europe and its Policy Learning Platform (PLP).

fr.name: Mediterranean innovation sustainable economy community
fr.description:
Community4Innovation est un projet de communauté thématique Interreg Euro-MED correspondant à la Mission 1 qui vise à renforcer l'économie durable innovante (EDI) dans en Méditerranée grâce au développement des capacités de recherche et d'innovation afin de faciliter la transition vers une économie circulaire et de consolider un écosystème d'innovation compétitif intégrant les parties prenantes de la quadruple hélice. Community4Innovation agit comme une communauté structurée qui renforce la collaboration entre projets, parties prenantes et institutions pour trouver des solutions aux défis communs et de promouvoir le transfert de nouvelles pratiques vers d'autres acteurs et territoires pour qu’ils les intègrent dans leurs politiques et leurs stratégies.
Les résultats comprennent une communauté de pratique visant l'apprentissage collectif entre projets thématiques (PT) par le biais de la coopération transnationale, ainsi qu’un hub autour duquel s’organise un solide réseau multisectoriel dans le domaine de l’EDI afin de faciliter les synergies et le transfert des solutions à toute la région méditerranéenne. Le projet développera aussi un catalogue de connaissances comprenant les résultats des PT dans le domaine de l'EDI, et mettra en place un programme de renforcement des capacités pour les réutilisateurs potentiels de ces derniers, en particulier les organisations de soutien aux entreprises, les ONG et les autorités publiques. Ce programme inclura des rencontres régulières pour répondre aux besoins tant top-down (UpM, PNUE/PAM, PDI, autorités régionales) que bottom-up (PT, utilisateurs finaux). Il mettra en place une coordination globale et collaborera avec les institutions compétentes pour contribuer à la mise en œuvre de l'agenda GreenerMed et de la Déclaration ministérielle de l'UpM sur l'économie bleue durable, ainsi que de la Stratégie méditerranéenne pour le développement durable et de la Stratégie à moyen terme 2022-2027 du PNUE/PAM pour la réalisation des ODD.
fr.expected_achievements:
Establish the conditions for the effective identification of actionable knowledge from thematic projects to facilitate the exploitation, sharing and reuse of results by Mission 1 Thematic Projects, by articulating a community of practice.
Uptake TPs’ knowledge and results by the Community of Practice enlarged to a wide range of stakeholders in the Mediterranean, to turn them into real practices at local, regional and national levels and encourage new public policies’ development. 
Increase the coordination among actors of the Mediterranean region (multi-level, transnational) based on shared knowledge, experience and results contributing to an Innovative Sustainable Economy for a climate-neutral and resilient society.
fr.actual_achievements:
Representation and active participation of all partners were achieved in 3 main coordination events: the LP seminar in Marseille, France (Jan, 2023); the C4I KoM in Vic, Spain (Feb, 2023); and, the online Mission Launch (Jul, 2023), which counted with up to 120 participants from 22 different countries. In addition, a total of 38 online technical meetings among different project actors’ groups have been carried out for a proper coordination.

Internal tools have been designed and approved among the consortium to achieve a well-structured coordination and management. These tools are the project manual, the dashboard, and the strategic plan. In addition, Social Media channels (LinkedIn and Twitter) were reactivated taking advantage of the followers already achieved by the previous Green Growth community. A Mission website has been launched (May, 2023) and regularly updated (specially the News & Events sections). The Mailchimp newsletter template for the mailings has been set. A Mission Communication Strategy has been created as a master guide to align the external communication of the two governance projects of the ISE Mission. Efforts from all partners were given to achieve a common understanding and alignment with D4I activities and conclude on a more detailed 2-y workplan and update the version of the original application form (Jun, 2023).

During the initial semester different project partners participated in several events to communicate about the project, maintain current collaborations, and explore new potential synergies. The partners participation to in-person events were done accordingly to their closest location. GSC attended the Community4LivingAreas KoM in Tirana, Albania (Mar, 2023). In turn, DVISION and MIO-ECSDE attended the Dialogue4Tourism KoM in Athenes, Greece (Mar, 2023). MIO-ECSDE represented the project participating in the round table ‘Innovative and Circular Tourism’. DVISION and CENER21 also participated in the 1st MED Innovation Camp organised by the Dialogue4Innovation in the frame of the 8th EUSAIR Forum in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina (May, 2023). The Forum aimed at reducing the innovation gap and addressing the Sustainable Development Goals, ensuring alignment with regional and European objectives. en.name: Mediterranean innovation sustainable economy community
en.description:
Community4Innovation is an Interreg Euro-MED Thematic Community Project under Mission 1. It aims at strengthening innovative sustainable economy (ISE) in the Mediterranean region by developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities for the uptake of technologies in order to support the transition to a circular economy and consolidate a competitive innovation ecosystem with the 4-helix stakeholders. Therefore, Community4Innovation acts as an engaged and structured community that improves collaboration among projects, stakeholders and relevant institutions to find solutions for common challenges and promote the transfer of novel practices to other actors and territories for their integration into policies and strategies.

Community4Innovation main outputs comprise the activation of a Community of Practice as a process of collective knowing and learning among projects through transnational cooperation, as well as a Hub aiming to establish a strong and multi-sectorial network in the field of ISE to amplify synergies and facilitate the replicability of solutions in the whole Mediterranean region. In addition, Community4Innovation will develop an interactive Catalogue of Actionable Knowledge with all available project’ results in the field of ISE and it will also generate a capacity-building and transferring programme for potential takers, in particular Business Support Organisation, NGOs and public authorities. This plan envisages recurrent encountering opportunities to tackle both top-down (i.e. UfM, UNEP/MAP, IDP, regional authorities) and bottom-up (i.e. TP, end-users) needs. To this end, an overall coordination will be set to ensure collaboration with relevant institutions and contribute to the implementation of the UfM GreenerMed Agenda and the UfM Ministerial Declaration on Sustainable Blue Economy, as well as to the Mediterranean Strategy for Sustainable Development and UNEP/MAP Medium term Strategy 2022-2027 for the overall achievement of the SDGs.
en.expected_achievements:
Establish the conditions for the effective identification of actionable knowledge from thematic projects to facilitate the exploitation, sharing and reuse of results by Mission 1, articulating a Community of Practice.
Uptake TPs' knowledge and results by the Community of Practice enlarged to a wide range of stakeholders in the Mediterranean, to turn them into real practices at local, regional and national levels and encourage new public policies' development.
Increase the coordination among actors of the Mediterranean region (multi-level, transnational) based on shared knowledge, experience and results contributing to an Innovative Sustainable Economy for a climate-neutral and resilient society.

en.name: Thematic Community project of the Interreg Euro-MED Sustainable Tourism Mission
en.description:
The project Community4Tourism, the Thematic Community project of the Interreg Euro-MED Sustainable Tourism Mission aims to contribute to the EU territorial cooperation and its transnational dimension through the objectives included in the Programme and the main goals of the Mission 4, related to the Enhancement of Sustainable Tourism in the MED Area and the transition towards a climate-neutral and resilient society.

This project is based upon the main objective of improving a better governance of cooperation, both policy governance and multi-sectoral, multi-level and transnational territorial governance, with a strong focus on environment and climate, promoting a smarter and greener Mediterranean, which is in line with the European Green Deal, the Sustainable Development Goals of the United Nations and the Territorial Agenda 2030. The project is built upon the outputs of the Interreg MED 14/20 Sustainable Tourism and BleuTourMed Projects but reinforces its objectives to current new challenges. 

It integrates the 4 Programme’s specific objectives, as tourism has shown to be a transversal issue in the MED area, involving all kinds of stakeholders, agents and territories. It will address its activities to pursue the consolidation of a competitive innovation ecosystem, to support a circular economy, to promote climate change adaptation, and to enhance nature and biodiversity. As a result of its activities, it will come up with jointly developed solutions and promote cooperation across borders. 

The project and the partnership are set up taking into consideration the transnational dimension for the success of the programme’s mission and project’s objectives. This approach will be maintained and ensured along the lifecycle of the project, as the partners will act as territorial antennas to ensure coverage of all the MED area and to maintain a smooth dialogue with all the territories, main actors and stakeholders, and keep an eye on its main needs and challenges.
en.expected_achievements:
Facilitate the exploitation, sharing and reuse of knowledge, experiences and project results by other Euro-MED projects or other programmes to foster the production of relevant work, and establish conditions to develop synergies between projects. 
Encourage the transfer of practices and results to other actors and territories and their integration in the development of local, regional, national and European (mainstream) policies and strategies.
Increase coordination between actors in the MED (multi-level, transnational) on the basis of the acquired knowledge, experience and results. Improve synergies responding to Mission4 challenges, contribute to the macro-regional and sea basin strategy.
en.actual_achievements:
C4T project has worked during the 1st period of implementation in establishing the basis for the internal coordination and the sound development of the work plan for the years to come. The partnership has also initiated preparatory actions to start with the implementation of some of the main activities planned. A common work and regular collaboration has been as well initiated in the framework of he two mission project’s expected coordination.

On an internal level, the partnership has developed its management and coordination rules (project handbook, steering committee rules of procedure, working tools…), has been holding several technical committee meetings and one steering committee meeting during the first semester (Marseille, on January). The partnership has started to work in their internal databases, at a project level, mission level and for the associated Partners. The project has worked further in the definition of the work plans of each work package and their respective activities. 

As mentioned, some preparatory actions for the implementation of the first project activities have been initiated: 

In the framework of WP1, the main characteristics of the Join the MED platform have been agreed, the definition and methodology of the thematic working groups activity has been drafted, and also the concept note for the SDG indicators performance report.
In the framework of WP2, C4T has progressed in the definition of the Mediterranean Clusters and their general management guidelines, which have been drafted and foreseen to be finalized by period 2. The handbook on the ICZM protocol related to tourism has also been produced although the activity will start in 2024.
In the framework of WP3, the terms of reference for the Blueprint for Regional Climate Action Plans have been drafted as well as a calendar and a specific work plan for this activity, and the activity will be initiated in period 2. The Joint Memorandum of Understanding between C4T and D4T has also been produced while it will be finalised and signed during period 2, and the 1st Joint Annual Forum (JAF) of the ST mission has been organised during this period and will take place in November 2023.

In terms of communication, C4T project has worked on the setting up of the own internal communication materials (documents, presentations, project poster, etc), while it has set up the common communication workflow among the two projects of the ST Mission. An editorial board among the two projects has been created to coordinate the needed actions on communication and an Action Plan for the launch of the social media channels has been drafted, with the contribution of D4T. At the mission level it has been launched the mission website, the launch of the social media channels and it has been set the basis for the Joint Communication Strategy, to be finalised at the beginning of the period 2. 

At the mission level, C4T has worked in the definition of a coordinated work plan together with D4T project, identifying synergies and contributions among both project’s activities. Several bilateral meetings have been held in the framework of each WP of both projects and different collaborations have been defined in the work plans. Both projects have also worked together on behalf of the communication of the mission, as this will be a common activity, which have included the creation of an editorial board for the joint collaboration the development of a joint coordination document for the communication of the mission, the design and launch of the mission website and the launch of the social media platforms that will be used by the mission. 

At the programme level, C4T has participated in the regular events of the Joint Secretariat, different preparation meetings, and offered feedback and information to what has been requested, liaised with other initiatives and actors such as the UfM. C4T also directly contributed to the organization of contents for different events, such as the multiprogramme coordination mechanism, the UfM Working Group on Environment or the programme’s event that will take place in Slovenia during the period 2. 

C4T project has worked during the 2nd period in the further establishment of the basis for the internal coordination and the coordination with the D4T project and in the initial development of the first activities to be implemented by the project. 

On an internal level, the partnership has finalised its steering committee rules of procedure, has been holding several technical committee meetings and one steering committee meeting (Volos, on July). The project organised its first C4T Associated Organisations meeting (organised online on the 5th of October 2023) to welcome the organisations to the Community, share the main activities of the project, and reinforce and promote their participation.

The partnership has kept updating its internal databases, mainly in the one referred to the Thematic Projects of the Community. C4T has kept using and updating the management tools such as a Google Drive, Basecamp and JEMS, and has taken more steps towards the final definition of a common solution for the GDPR management within the project and in the context of the collaboration with the IDP project (shared communication tools, etc).

Related to the project’s activities, the following actions have been developed: 

In the framework of WP1, C4T has elaborated internal draft methodologies for the organization and development of the Working Groups and the mapping of synergies, which will be completed during the Period 3. C4T has progressed with the development of Join the Med platform and has participated in scientific conferences for the collective dissemination and valorisation of the projects’ results. 

In the framework of WP2, the general management guidelines of the Mediterranean Clusters have been completed. The partnership has worked on the MED Cluster composition after the publication of the approved thematic projects in November and after the event with the Aos, whose preferences were considered. C4T selected an external expert for the development of the ICZM protocol at regional level.

In the framework of WP3, C4T continued to ensure coordination between the TCP and the IDP, in particular through the development and signing of a MoU, formalising the coordination process between the two governance projects, signed by the lead partners of the two tourism mission governance projects at the 1st Joint Annual Forum held in Ljubjana on 22 November 2023. In addition, C4T has begun to work on the development of the "Blueprint for Regional Climate Action Plans", selecting an expert in August that started its preparation during the last semester.

In terms of dissemination and participation to external events, C4T project and the Sustainable Tourism Mission have been presented in the following events and activities: D4T Steering Committee Meeting, online (04.10.2023); CPMR-IMC Task Force on culture and tourism, online (06.10.2023); Tourism Innovation Summit, Sevilla (18.10.2023); Connecting the dots: the path to a smarter and greener Mediterranean, Slovenia (21-22.11.2023); EPM Mediterranean Days, online (28.11.2023); and the UfM MED Talks, online (05.12.2023). C4T also liaised through its partners acting as project antennas with Euro-MED organisations and initiatives (WestMED and UfM events).

In terms of communication, a social media campaign has been launched highlighting the results of the previous period results and the main issues at Mediterranean level concerning the topic of Tourism. C4T has developed communication materials for different events (such as Slovenia, the Joint Annual Forum, MED Talks event, etc). A communication unit of both governance projects has been set up, which agreed on the Mission workflow at the communication level, and the Joint Communication Strategy was finalized. C4T prepared and published some news on the website, took care of updating the visual identity, when necessary, and published posts on the Mission social media channels in line with the calendar shared with D4T.

At the mission and programme levels, C4T has coordinated with the IDP and the JS for the development of joint activities and complementarities through all Work Packages. The 1st Joint Annual Forum, which will take place annualy, was organized in the framework of the programme’s event in Slovenia, on the 22nd of November, in which C4T also actively participated during the event and in the organization phase. C4T has been actively using the Basecamp tool to coordinate with the Secretariat and the rest of governance projects whenever it was requested.




In the 3rd period of implementation, C4T project has successfully gone through the foreseen activities, with an excellent level of coordination and participation among the partnership and in coordination with the D4T project, the Euro-MED4GOV group and the JS.

The 3rd period was characterized by the start of the first Thematic Projects approved and, therefore, the organization of the Kick-off meeting of the Sustainable Tourism Community (Rome, 10-11 April), the official starting point of the work and the collaboration with the Thematic Projects of the Mission, together with the governance projects of Mission Sustainable Tourism and other external stakeholders and Associated Partners, and the opportunity to meet the thematic projects, share, and initiate the main activities with them.

At a coordination / management level, the partnership held 7 internal technical meetings and 1 steering committee (Rome, 11 April), in which all partners were involved in the delivery of the different activities of the work plan, exchanged points of view and assumed their respective responsibilities. The partnership has kept updating its internal databases, mainly the one referred to the TPs. C4T has kept using the management tools such as a Google Drive, Basecamp and JEMS, and has taken more steps towards the solution for the GDPR management within the project and in the context of the collaboration with the IDP project (shared communication tools, etc).

Related to the project’s activities, the following actions have been developed: 

In the framework of WP1, C4T submitted an abstract under the ERSA Congress, elaborated on the methodology for establishing solid exchanges and synergies through custom made mapping templates and draft matrices, updated and launched the Join the Med platform and disseminated it inside and outside the Community. In the framework of the monitoring of the Community and the Thematic Projects, all partners organised initial meetings with their respective TPs to follow-up (using the distribution and the roles of the “Angels”), an activity that will be maintained over the life of the projects. All partners participated in the KoM and SC of their assigned TPs.

In the framework of WP2, the first activities of the MED Clusters were organised, with the contracting of 3 external experts to provide orientation papers to guide and inspire the activities of each cluster, and the celebration of 1st annual meeting of the MED Clusters (Innovation, Circular Economy, Climate Change & Nature, and Biodiversity), which took place during the KoM of the Community in Rome and which were animated by the same experts that prepared the papers. An action plan for each cluster is being prepared, to be finalized in the next semester. All C4T partners are involved in these activities through their roles of MED Clusters coordinators.

C4T has supervised the drafting of the “Best Practices Manual: Unpacking the Potential of ICZM/MSP Processes for Planning Sustainable Tourism Activities in the Mediterranean”, developed by an external expert. The manual will be published in Period 4.
