C4T has also started organizing the 2nd Joint Annual Forum of the ST Mission in coordination with D4T, to take place during period 4. C4T has continued to ensure coordination between the TCP and the IDP, through monthly bilateral meetings by the coordination WP leaders, and with the overview and participation of the LPs as needed. 

In terms of dissemination, C4T engaged during this period mainly in the events related to the Thematic Projects (bilateral meetings, SC meetings and KoM), and has also participated in external/liaising events, such as: meetings in FITUR, Urban Agenda ST partnership Meeting, Natural Heritage Mission Working Groups, D4T Steering Committee Meeting, CPMR-IMC Culture and Sustainable Tourism Task Force, etc. C4T liaised with APs, regions, and other external stakeholders through some of its activities and through networks like CPMR and NECSTouR.

In terms of communication, several news and events were shared through the mission’s website and the SSMM channels. The communication team supported the publication of key documents such as the orientation papers of the MED Clusters, a brochure for the dissemination of the activity or the Blueprint (published in Period 4). The KoM was disseminated in the SSMM networks, as well as a campaign supporting the dissemination of the TPs. Other related and interesting news about sustainable tourism are being shared regularly.

In the framework of WP1, C4T has participated in scientific conferences for the collective dissemination and valorisation of the projects’ results, focusing mainly on the past projects' results but will gradually incorporate the results generated by the current thamtic projects. C4T has also elaborated on the methodologies for the organization and development of the Working Groups, the mapping of synergies and the contribution to UN SGDs indicators. Moreover, C4T has progressed with the development of Join the Med platform, by updating it and creating a direct access from the Mission's website, and has been coordinating with the IDP and the JS for the development of joint activities.  

In the framework of WP2, the activities have been centered mainly around the development and implementation of the MED Clusters activity: the project delivered the three Action Plans for the 3 Clusters and an online meeting for each cluster (15, 25 and 26 November 2024) was organised. 
Concerning the MED Academy a specific WG is working on the joint development of the activity at governance projects level, with the participacion of C4T representatives (UNIMED, DIBA, AIE and UTH). 
The Manual for the implementation of IZCM Protocol (D2.2.1) was delivered in November and the document will be also translated into French. There is still the need to plan the Practices Handbook Workshop. 

In the framework of WP3, C4T continued to ensure coordination between the TCP and the IDP, in particular through the development and signing of a MoU, formalising the coordination process between the two governance projects, signed by the lead partners of the two tourism mission governance projects at the 1st Joint Annual Forum held in Ljubjana on 22 November 2023. In addition, C4T has begun to work on the development of the "Blueprint for Regional Climate Action Plans", selecting an expert in August that started its preparation during the last semester.

In terms of communication, C4T has coordinated the activities with D4T with regards to the posts in the social media and the updates of the webiste. The communication teams has supported the different WP leaders and the dissemination of the activities of the project, the mission and the thematic projects itself.

C4T has also participated inseveral internal and external events. On the one hand, the C4T partners have attended to tehe meetings of TPs in order to follow-up the implementation of their activities. The partners have also participated in different external events supporting the dissemination of the activities of the Mission, events such as: Euro-MED4GOV meetings (03-04.07.2024, Marseille and 14.11.2024, online), Forum Turistic (03.10.2024, Barcelona), Medaweek 2024 (29-30.10.2024, Barcelona), Sun&Blue Congress: 2nd Joint annual Forum, WestMED Technical Group on Sustainable Tourism & CPMR/IMC Task Force on Sustainable Tourism (20-22.11.2024, Almería), Smart Tourism Congress (27.11.2024, Barcelona), MedLab Thematic Session on Sustainable Tourism (9.12.2024, Online) and the UfM MED TALKS 2nd Edition (16.12.2024, Online).

In the 5th period of implementation, C4T project has successfully gone through the foreseen activities, with a good level of coordination and participation among the partnership and in coordination with the D4T project, the Euro-MED4GOV group and the JS.

The 5th period was characterized by the integration of 2 new rounds of projects to the Community (10 new Thematic Projects + 2 Strategic Territorial Projects), which will determine the foreseen upcoming activities of C4T and feed and enrich the work of the Sustainable Tourism Mission. At the beginning of the Period 5, C4T also reviewed its Application Form and aligned with D4T in the development of some common activities. 

C4T partnership has organised regular Steering Committee and Technical Meetings to work on the implementation of the diferent activities and has participated in regular coordination meetings with D4T, the JS and other govenance projects of the programme. C4T partnership held also bilateral meetings with all the current projects for the monitoring of their activities.

In the framework of WP1, C4T submitted abstracts for scientific publications, elaborated on the methodologies for the organization and development of the Working Groups, the mapping of synergies and the contribution to UN SGDs indicators. C4T kept updating Join the Med platform, integrating results and working on its positioning and visibility. 

Under WP2, C4T has worked on analyses of the replicability conditions of project results  and kept advancing with the Action Plan of the MED Clusters through the organisation of a new online meetings (April), the delivery of the reports, the preparation for the upcoming annual Meeting (Period 6) and the redefinition of the objectives for this activity. A Capacity Building Workshop focused on the Practices Handbook will also be held at the Community’s annual meeting, for which some preparatory actions have been held. Concerning the MED Academy, the partners and specifically UNIMED participate in the joint development of the activity with the JS. 

In the framework of WP3, C4T delivered three interactive 90-minute Capacity Building sessions dedicated to the Blueprint for Tourism Climate Action Plans, with a huge success and wide participation (+230), a report has been prepared. Some preparatory actions and meetings have been iundertaken for the upcoming 3rd Joint Annual Forum (to be held in the framework of the Interreg Euro-MED event in Varna, Period 6), together with D4T. C4T continued engaging with key actors mainly through CPMR and NECSTouR networks during this period. Finally, the Carbon Footprint of the project is being monitored period by period.

With regards to the coordination with D4T project and some of their activities, C4T has collaborated closely, supporting the coordinated review of the D4T White Paper, contributing to the development of the D4T MOOC on mainstreaming (coordinated by Région Sud), and supporting the relaunch of the MED NSTO, among other joint actions. C4T also reviewed the 2-year workplan making sure to coordinate activities between the 2 projects as foreseen in their joint MoU.

In terms of communication, C4T has coordinated the activities with D4T with regards to the posts in the social media and the updates of the webiste. The communication teams has supported the different WP leaders and the dissemination of the activities of the project, the mission and the thematic projects itself. C4T has also produced and updated different materials and publications such as: the Practices Handbook, the report of the online sessions of the Blueprint, the update of MED Clusters brochure, the project and the Mission’s powerpoint presentations used in several events, etc.

C4T participated as well in different relevant external events, such as: CAT4Governance Euro-MED 2025 in Vic (March), WestMED Advisory Group meeting on sustainable tourism (Feb), Urban Agenda Partnership Meeting (Feb), Ufm Blue Economy Platform (March), NECSTouR AGM (May), “Paths That Last” Mediterranean Multi-programme Mechanism event (June), the Cross-Webinar with BlueMissionMed (July).

fr.name: Thematic Community project of the Interreg Euro-MED Sustainable Tourism Mission
fr.description:
Le projet Community4Tourism, l'Euro-MED Communauté pour le Tourisme Durable entend contribuer à la coopération territoriale de l'UE et à sa dimension transnationale à travers les objectifs spécifiques inclus dans le programme et les objectifs de la Mission 4, liés à l’amélioration du tourisme durable dans l'espace MEDet à la transition vers une société neutre et résiliente.

Ce projet a pour objectif principal de favoriser une meilleure gouvernance de la coopération multisectorielle, multiniveau et transnationale, en mettant l'accent sur l'environnement et le climat, en accord avec le Green Deal européen, les Objectifs de Développement Durable des Nations Unies et l'Agenda Territorial 2030. Le projet s'appuie sur les résultats des projets Interreg MED 2014-2020 Tourisme Durable et BleuTourMed, mais renforce ses objectifs pour répondre aux nouveaux défis actuels.

Le projet intègre les 4 objectifs spécifiques du Programme, car le tourisme s'est une question transversale dans l'espace méditerranéen, impliquant toutes sortes d'acteurs, d'agents et de territoires. Le projet mènera ses activités dans le but de consolider un écosystème d'innovation compétitif, de soutenir une économie circulaire, de promouvoir l'adaptation au changement climatique, et de valoriser la nature et la biodiversité. Grâce à ses activités, proposera des solutions élaborées conjointement et encouragera la coopération transfrontalière.

Le projet et le partenariat sont mis en place en tenant compte de la dimension transnationale nécessaire à la réussite de la mission du programme et des objectifs du projet. Cette approche sera maintenue et assurée tout au long du cycle de vie du projet, les partenaires agiront comme des antennes territoriales pour assurer la couverture de toute la zone méditerranéenne et pour maintenir un dialogue fluide avec tous les territoires, les principaux acteurs et parties prenantes, et garder un œil sur ses principaux besoins et défis.
fr.expected_achievements:
Facilitate the exploitation, sharing and reuse of knowledge, experiences and project results by other Euro-MED projects or other programmes to foster the production of relevant work, and establish conditions to develop synergies between projects.  
Encourage the transfer of practices and results to other actors and territories and their integration in the development of local, regional, national and European (mainstream) policies and strategies.
Increase coordination between actors in the MED (multi-level, transnational) on the basis of the acquired knowledge, experience and results. Improve synergies responding to Mission4 challenges, contribute to the macro-regional and sea basin strategy.

C4T kept updating Join the Med platform, integrating results and working on its positioning and visibility. 

Under WP2, C4T has worked on analyses of the replicability conditions of project results  and kept advancing with the Action Plan of the MED Clusters through the organisation of a new online meetings (April), the delivery of the reports, the preparation for the upcoming annual Meeting (Period 6) and the redefinition of the objectives for this activity. A Capacity Building Workshop focused on the Practices Handbook will also be held at the Community’s annual meeting, for which some preparatory actions have been held. Concerning the MED Academy, the partners and specifically UNIMED participate in the joint development of the activity with the JS. 

In the framework of WP3, C4T delivered three interactive 90-minute Capacity Building sessions dedicated to the Blueprint for Tourism Climate Action Plans, with a huge success and wide participation (+230), a report has been prepared. Some preparatory actions and meetings have been iundertaken for the upcoming 3rd Joint Annual Forum (to be held in the framework of the Interreg Euro-MED event in Varna, Period 6), together with D4T. C4T continued engaging with key actors mainly through CPMR and NECSTouR networks during this period. Finally, the Carbon Footprint of the project is being monitored period by period.

With regards to the coordination with D4T project and some of their activities, C4T has collaborated closely, supporting the coordinated review of the D4T White Paper, contributing to the development of the D4T MOOC on mainstreaming (coordinated by Région Sud), and supporting the relaunch of the MED NSTO, among other joint actions. C4T also reviewed the 2-year workplan making sure to coordinate activities between the 2 projects as foreseen in their joint MoU.

In terms of communication, C4T has coordinated the activities with D4T with regards to the posts in the social media and the updates of the webiste. The communication teams has supported the different WP leaders and the dissemination of the activities of the project, the mission and the thematic projects itself. C4T has also produced and updated different materials and publications such as: the Practices Handbook, the report of the online sessions of the Blueprint, the update of MED Clusters brochure, the project and the Mission’s powerpoint presentations used in several events, etc.

C4T participated as well in different relevant external events, such as: CAT4Governance Euro-MED 2025 in Vic (March), WestMED Advisory Group meeting on sustainable tourism (Feb), Urban Agenda Partnership Meeting (Feb), Ufm Blue Economy Platform (March), NECSTouR AGM (May), “Paths That Last” Mediterranean Multi-programme Mechanism event (June), the Cross-Webinar with BlueMissionMed (July).
